notes: 12-microbe Phase 1 catalog. First 6 are canonical DN cast per Docs/HANDOFF_FROM_LABSMITH_DISTRIBUTED_NARRATIVE_RETROFIT.md (Wave 32b). Remaining 6 are codex-supporting microbes (beneficial-first framing per CLAUDE.md trauma-informed posture). Names checked against labsmith REGISTRY_PORTFOLIO_CHARACTER_NAMES.md — supporting microbes use scientific-genus-flavored slugs only.

microbes.catchphrase: Friend in your food. Friend in your gut.
microbes.factCard: Lactobacillus species ferment milk into yogurt and live in your large intestine, where they often help with digestion.
Saccharomyces cerevisiae turn sugars into carbon dioxide bubbles — that's what makes bread dough rise.

microbes.catchphrase: Sunlight. Then air. Then everything else.
microbes.factCard: Cyanobacteria use sunlight to make their own food and release oxygen. They helped create the oxygen in Earth's atmosphere.
Mycorrhizal fungi link tree roots underground, often helping plants share water and nutrients across long distances.

microbes.catchphrase: Some friends. Some not. All real.
microbes.factCard: Some bacteria can cause illness when they grow too large in number, but most of them live with us peacefully.

microbes.catchphrase: I check IDs. Patient and careful.
microbes.factCard: Your immune cells — like macrophages and B-cells — patiently check every microbe they meet and learn which ones to trust.
Bifidobacterium species feast on the fiber in fruits and vegetables — one of the gut's most helpful families.
Akkermansia muciniphila feeds on the gut's mucus layer and helps keep the intestinal wall strong.
Many Streptococcus species live quietly in the mouth and throat — a few can cause illness, but most are part of normal mouth life.

microbes.catchphrase: Most of us help. A few don't.
microbes.factCard: Most Escherichia coli strains live peacefully in your gut and help make vitamin K. Only a few specific strains can make people sick.

microbes.catchphrase: I am very small. I prefer noses.
microbes.factCard: Rhinoviruses are tiny viruses that often cause the common cold. They are the most common reason kids sniffle in winter.

microbes.catchphrase: I survive what nothing else can.
microbes.factCard: Deinococcus radiodurans is an extremophile that survives radiation, cold, and dehydration that would defeat most life on Earth.
